official_gazette.name: UAE Official Gazette (Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah)
official_gazette.notes: Issued by Ministry of Justice. Full archive online since 1971.

consolidated.name: UAE Legislation Portal
consolidated.notes: Federal laws, decree-laws, cabinet resolutions. Bilingual.

unblock_hints: Federal MOJ portal is cloudflare-WAF gated; pursue MOU with MOJ Open Data team rather than scraping
Bayanat (data.bayanat.ae) sometimes hosts MOJ datasets — check periodically
SCA API requires CSRF auth — request a public API key via SCA contact form
TDRC tax appeals are protected by Tax Procedures Law Art 7 confidentiality — unscrapeable by design
AE.notes: Free zones (DIFC, ADGM) have the best open access. Federal is the hard part.

consolidated.name: LLOC (Legislation and Legal Opinion Commission)
consolidated.notes: lloc.gov.bh = legalaffairs.gov.bh (same operator). DNS currently unresolvable from non-BH ranges.

key_regulators: Central Bank of Bahrain - cbb.gov.bh (rulebook on Thomson Reuters - paywalled)

unblock_hints: Try resolving lloc.gov.bh from a Bahraini residential proxy
CBB rulebook is on a TR subdomain; only working path is direct partnership with CBB
ccb.bh returns 403; try via google cache + archive.org
data.gov.bh has zero legal datasets — file a request issue
BH.notes: One of the worst access situations in the GCC despite small corpus size.

unblock_hints: Kuwait National Assembly (kna.kw) publishes draft + enacted laws
Kuwait Bar Association archives may be the most complete unofficial source
KW.notes: HAQQ has limited GCC presence outside UAE/SA — Kuwait is low-customer-density.

official_gazette.name: Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah
official_gazette.notes: Run by Ministry of Justice and Legal Affairs.

unblock_hints: Oman is the cleanest GCC case — qanoon.om is well-structured
Extend existing scrapers to capture Royal Decrees archive back to 1971
OM.notes: Strong starting point for "GCC complete" milestone.

official_gazette.name: Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah
official_gazette.notes: Run by Ministry of Justice. Bilingual.

unblock_hints: Al Meezan is well-structured — almost a model portal for the region
QFC has separate English-language regime; combine for full coverage
QA.notes: Best-organized civil-law portal in the GCC.

consolidated.name: Bureau of Experts (BoE) Laws Portal
consolidated.operator: Council of Ministers Bureau of Experts

consolidated.name: National Center for Archives and Records (NCAR)

key_regulators: General Authority of Zakat and Tax (ZATCA)

unblock_hints: laws.boe.gov.sa is THE source for codified statutes — pursue scraping with Saudi residential proxy
Hijri-Gregorian conversion is non-trivial — must implement both ways
MOJ portal (moj.gov.sa) publishes Supreme Court rulings since 2017 reform — high priority
Najiz (najiz.sa) e-court service has public-decisions endpoint behind login wall
SAMA rulebook is public PDF — clean win
SDAIA has its own AI/data laws portal — newly relevant
SA.notes:
Highest-impact gap in the entire region. SA is a top-3 HAQQ ICP market and
has ZERO working scrapers in upstream. Hijri calendar handling is the
technical wedge that excludes most non-MENA contributors.

unblock_hints: Manshurat is the de facto national legal database — extend coverage from current Legal Corpus scraper
Court of Cassation digital archive needs Arabic-OCR pipeline for pre-2010 decisions
SCC publishes rulings PDF — relatively clean
EG.notes: Largest Arabic-speaking legal system. High-value target.

official_gazette.name: Al-Waqāʾiʿ al-ʿIrāqiyyah
official_gazette.notes: Published by MOJ. KRG has separate gazette in Erbil.

consolidated.name: KRG Legal Database
consolidated.notes: Kurdistan Regional Government legislation in Kurdish + Arabic.

unblock_hints: iraqld.hjc.iq is the obvious starting point — test whether captcha is bypassable
KRG corpus is a separate scraper — don't conflate with federal
Pre-2003 Saddam-era laws still in force in many areas — needs historical coverage
IQ.notes:
Politically loaded — KRG vs Federal authority is contested. Abbas/Sherif
review required before publishing. Treat as two parallel jurisdictions.

unblock_hints: lob.jo is the official source — extend to capture regulations and PM decisions
Jordan E-Government has bilingual subset worth indexing
JO.notes: One of the more tractable Mashriq jurisdictions.

official_gazette.name: Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah (Journal Officiel de la République Libanaise)
official_gazette.notes: Issued by Presidency of Council of Ministers. Weekly. Digital archive incomplete pre-2003.

consolidated.name: LegiLiban (parliament-linked)
consolidated.notes: Currently DNS-unreachable. May have moved or been deprecated.

consolidated.name: USJ Legal Informatics (CEDROMA)
consolidated.notes: Saint Joseph University academic mirror, often more reliable than official sources.

key_regulators: Personal status courts (18 confessional regimes — non-codified)

unblock_hints.First fix: ping LegiLiban current status via archive.org and USJ contacts

unblock_hints: PCM gazette PDFs are the most stable primary source — direct PDF crawl works
Partner with USJ CEDROMA (Saint Joseph University academic mirror) for a maintained data feed
Personal status laws are NOT codified — needs special treatment (out-of-scope for v1)
LB.notes:
Stephane's diaspora wedge. Zero working scrapers + critical HAQQ ICP +
Stephane's direct network. Strongest single bet in the entire Atlas.

official_gazette.name: Al-Waqāʾiʿ al-Filasṭīniyyah
official_gazette.notes: Issued by PA in West Bank. Gaza has separate Hamas-issued gazette.

consolidated.name: Al-Muqtafi
consolidated.notes: De facto national legal database. Academic, well-maintained.

unblock_hints: Al-Muqtafi (Birzeit) is the academic gold standard — partner directly with Institute of Law
Layered legal system means citation conventions vary by era — needs explicit period tagging
PA vs Hamas legislation divergence post-2007 needs explicit handling
PS.notes:
Politically loaded. Abbas/Sherif review required. Academic partnership with
Birzeit is the highest-leverage path.

unblock_hints: Parliament site is the only viable primary source
Sanctions exposure — confirm with Abbas before publishing scraper or hosting corpus
Post-Dec 2024 transition may invalidate or amend large parts of the corpus — track explicitly
SY.notes:
Currently in legal transition (post Dec 2024). Corpus is unstable —
legitimacy of pre-transition laws is contested. Atlas should explicitly
timestamp Syrian sources.

official_gazette.name: Journal Officiel (Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah)
official_gazette.notes: Run by Secrétariat Général du Gouvernement.

unblock_hints: DGI tax portal returns 500 — wait for govt to fix or pivot to MOF gazette
Coverage already strong — focus on case law completeness (CourSupreme has WordPress API)
DZ.notes: Strongest Maghreb coverage in upstream. Maintain rather than build.

official_gazette.name: Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah
official_gazette.notes: Bifurcated state — Tripoli (GNU) and Benghazi (HoR) issue separate gazettes.

unblock_hints: Bifurcation must be explicit — two parallel corpora since 2014
DCAF (Geneva Centre for Security Sector Governance) Libya page is best non-official aggregator
LY.notes: Same political-instability caveats as Syria. Treat as two jurisdictions.

official_gazette.name: Bulletin Officiel (Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah)
official_gazette.notes: Issued by Secrétariat Général du Gouvernement.

unblock_hints: SGG Bulletin Officiel PDF archive is the gold standard primary source
Adala has decent coverage but inconsistent metadata — needs normalization
MA.notes: Comparatively easy. Maintain + extend rather than rebuild.

unblock_hints: Smallest corpus in MENA — entire codified law set fits in a few hundred PDFs
SGG is reachable but slow — use polite rate limit
MR.notes: Small but tractable. Low priority unless completionism mandates it.

official_gazette.name: Journal Officiel de la République Tunisienne (JORT)

unblock_hints: JORT site has been stable for years — direct PDF crawl
Post-2021 political situation has produced procedural irregularities — flag as needed

official_gazette.name: Al-Jarīdah al-Rasmīyah
official_gazette.notes: Bifurcated state — IRG (Aden, internationally recognized) and Houthi-controlled (Sanaa) issue parallel laws since 2014/2015.

consolidated.name: Ministry of Legal Affairs
consolidated.notes: Currently of uncertain status given conflict.

apex_courts: Supreme Court (de jure single, de facto bifurcated)
key_regulators: Central Bank of Yemen - bifurcated between Aden and Sanaa branches

unblock_hints: File "New Source" issue upstream — Yemen has zero coverage
Pre-2014 unified corpus is the most realistic v1 target
Post-2014 parallel corpora need explicit IRG/Houthi labeling
Yemeni Bar Association archives may be the most complete remaining record
University of Sanaa Law School historically maintained academic mirrors
YE.notes:
The ONLY MENA country with zero entries in worldwidelaw. Greenfield
contribution opportunity, but politically and operationally hard.

unblock_hints: Pre-2019 corpus is most legally certain
Civil war ongoing — most authorities offline
University of Khartoum Faculty of Law historically maintained academic archives

official_gazette.name: Faafinta Rasmiga ah ee Jamhuuriyadda Federaalka Soomaaliya

unblock_hints: Federal vs Federal Member State (Puntland, Somaliland) divergence is structural
Xeer customary law is uncodified — out-of-scope for v1
Diaspora law schools (Hargeisa, Mogadishu Univ) maintain partial archives
SO.notes: Lowest priority. Cover only after every other MENA jurisdiction is at v1.

official_gazette.name: Journal Officiel de la République de Djibouti

unblock_hints: Smallest viable MENA corpus
Already covered upstream — maintain only

official_gazette.name: Journal Officiel de l'Union des Comores

unblock_hints: Tiny corpus — fits in a few hundred PDFs
Often shared with Comorian diaspora at La Reunion (French) — French sources may mirror
KM.notes: Skip in v1 unless completionism demands it.

consolidated.name: Qavanin (DOTIC)
consolidated.operator: Office of the President - Center for Codification of Laws

unblock_hints: Solar Hijri (Jalali) calendar conversion is the technical wedge
Sanctions exposure for hosting Iranian legal data — confirm with Abbas
DOTIC qavanin.ir is the gold-standard official source
Farsi language requires separate NLP pipeline from Arabic
IR.notes:
Sanctions and language barrier make this politically and technically
distinct from Arab MENA. Often excluded from "Arab Legal Atlas" framing.
Decision required: include or carve out into separate sub-project.

apex_courts: Supreme Rabbinical Court (personal status for Jews)

unblock_hints: Knesset publishes laws under permissive terms — direct PDF/HTML crawl
Supreme Court rulings via gov.il are publicly accessible but anti-bot protected
Nevo is the dominant commercial database — out-of-scope for open data
Hebrew-RTL processing is the technical wedge
IL.notes:
Politically sensitive in MENA framing. Stephane's stated peace-first
posture suggests including without commentary. Abbas review required.

unblock_hints: Best-covered MENA country in upstream — maintain rather than build
Anti-bot on a few courts can likely be bypassed with proper UA + rate limiting
TR.notes:
Whether Turkey is "MENA" is contested. Included here because HAQQ has
Turkish-speaking customers and Turkey is in the regional regulatory
conversation.
